name: Viru Gate Quarter
description: Eastern Lower Town quarter inside Viru Gate: the town wall and moat on the east, Muurivahe lane hugging the wall, Viru street running west toward Vana turg, St. Catherine's Passage and the Dominican monastery to the north, with Kalev's smithy, the Foaming Mug brewery, the public well, and the Viru Watch gate post.

phase_states.description: Patrols and neighbors move through the street before the night consequence.
Watch pressure and crowd noise increase after the brewery commission resolves.

source_notes.summary: Street topology follows the real eastern Old Town plan: Viru Gate with flanking towers and moat, Viru street toward Vana turg, Muurivahe inside the wall, Vene street, St. Catherine's Passage, and the Dominican monastery of St. Catherine (founded 1246).
Individual houses, the smithy work yard against the wall, and the unnamed wall towers are composites; the later Hellemann tower (post-1343) is represented only as a generic wall tower.
